title: QuikChange Mutagenesis
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"eke58","text":"Primers","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"40o4a","text":"I use enzyme x to work with DNA sequences, it is installed on all the macs and has nice translation and reverse complement functions. Many of the same functions are available in a plasmid editor , which has Mac, Windows, and Linux versions. Choose a codon for the new amino acid that is the most similar to the original codon. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1rs5q","text":"      -Forward Primer ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2vap8","text":"          -identify the bases that code for your residue of interest (eg. 295-297) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f499e","text":"          -A - Copy the 10 bases before the codon (eg. 285-294) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8j6d8","text":"          -B - Write your new codon after the 10 bases you just copied ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6t7jp","text":"          -C - Copy the 22 bases that follow the codon (eg. 298-319), I always terminate in a G or C your forward primer should be 5'-A-B-C-3' ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4f3r3","text":"      -Reverse Primer ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cn43c","text":"          -D - Copy the ten bases after the codon (eg. 298-307) - then reverse complement it! ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":66,"length":28}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"l6gn","text":"          -E - Write your new codon - then reverse complement it!","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":38,"length":27}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8ssch","text":"          -F - Copy the 22 bases that precede the codon (eg. 273-294) - then reverse complement it! your reverse primer should be 5'-D-E-F-3' ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":72,"length":27}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fafci","text":"      -Order your primers from ElimBio (The newest PO B000177051) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"695l9","text":"      -They should arrive in 200uM concentration, make a 20uM stock by diluting 5ul of Elim Primer with 45ul of dH2O ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fc3sj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4nhg2","text":"PCR","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":3}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3tvrv","text":"Recipe","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9kvc7","text":"      -Recipe 1.25ul of 20uM Primer F ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4aemg","text":"      -1.25ul of 20uM Primer R ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"en4rr","text":"      -10ul Phusion Buffer (5x) 1ul of 10mM dNTPs ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"frvr1","text":"      -0.5ul Template DNA (from miniprep, preferably from DH5alpha cells, but BL21 is still dam+ so it should be fine - 5ng starting template is plenty! (note: good range = 50 - 100 ng) ) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ekkvs","text":"      -0.5ul Phusion polymerase ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5kdmm","text":"      -35ul H20 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e8hdu","text":"Cycle","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"baemt","text":"      -98C for 30s 98C for 5s (I usually do this step for 10 seconds) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a4378","text":"      -53C for 20s ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"40kto","text":"      -72C for 20s/kb (usually plasmids are ~7kb = 2:20)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4ml8p","text":"          -Cycle 16 times - more cycles are actually bad!","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":28,"length":29}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8g3cl","text":"      -72C for 8:00 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4arck","text":"      -4C for hold","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"43pho","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"784po","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2bvgl","text":"If it does not work:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cl90l","text":"      -Miniprep more colonies... ;) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ahcfo","text":"      -Order PAGE purified primers if you haven't already. steps: Prepare PCR mixture. PCR Mixture for QuickChange Mutagenesis by James Fraser

Perform the PCR using the following cycling conditions: -98C for 30s -98C for 5s (I usually do this step for 10 seconds) -53C for 20s -72C for 20s/kb (usually plasmids are ~7kb = 2:20) -Cycle 16 times - more cycles are actually bad! -72C for 8:00 -4C for hold
After PCR, add 1ul of DpnI to each pcr tube.
Incubate 1 hour-O/N at 37C. 15:00:00 I always do this O/N or for at least 4 hours (Avi). I got 95% efficiency when I DpnI ~24hrs (Cat).
PCR Purify using Qaigen kit. QIAquick PCR Purification Kit Qiagen Catalog #28104
Add 5ul of PCR reaction into 25ul DH5alpha or TG1 cells. I eluted in 27ul EB and transformed all 25ul elution in 50ul TG1 cells + 25ul KCM (Cat).
Incubate on ice for 5 minutes. 00:05:00 If you have time, incubate for 30min - 1hr (Avi).
Add 200ul LB (if in 96 well or 8 strip format) to each reaction. 350ul SOC, plate 50ul (Cat).
creator: James Fraser
